title: Ervatamia hexagona
taxonomy: Ervatamia hexagona is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is also known as a member of the genus Ervatamia, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical regions. Within the Apocynaceae family, Ervatamia hexagona is classified as a type of liana or climbing plant. The classification of Ervatamia hexagona reflects its unique characteristics and growth habits. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Ervatamia hexagona is closely related to other plants such as the genus Plumeria.
uses: Ervatamia hexagona is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various ailments. Its roots are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
